ead.archdesc:
======= RMM02399Elias W. Cady papers2399Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
February 20162B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
rghis geneo religThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:27:26 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Elias W. Cady papers
Cady, Elias W., 1792-
2399
54 items. 54 items.
1801-1857. Correspondence, legal and financial documents, and manuscripts. Letters to Cady and deeds, receipts, inventories, sermons, and poems. Collection material in English 2079963
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
February 2016
EAD encoding:
Marcie Farwell, February 2016
Date modified:
RMC Staff, February 2016
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Farmer.
Farmer in Dryden, New York and New York assemblyman, 1850 and 1857.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Letters to Cady relate to sheep raising, wool sales, lumbering, farm machinery, land sales, legislative issues including civil and criminal codes, free common schools, an agricultural college, the Sodus Canal, telegraphy, temperance, and family matters. There are also deeds, receipts, inventories, sermons, and poems. Correspondents include Jedediah Barber, Ezra Cornell, John McGraw, Henry W. Sage, Josiah B. Williams, and others.
Cite As:
Elias W. Cady papers, #2399. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Sermons. Poems. Inventories. Deeds. New York (State) -- Politics and government. Dryden (N.Y.) Temperance. Telegraph. Sheep. Real property, Exchange of. Lumbering. Logging. Canals. Agricultural colleges. Barber, Jedediah, 1787-1876. Cornell, Ezra, 1807-1874. McGraw, John, 1815-1877. Sage, Henry W. (Henry Williams), 1814-1897. Williams, Josiah B. (Josiah Butler), 1810-1883.
2079963 <<<<<<< HEAD Link to a partial finding aid.
Scope and Contents
All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.Series I. Correspondence - Personal and FamilySarah Child to Mehitabel Bramhall1801-09-021
Scope and Contents
Daughter Sarah Child of Stamford to My Honored Mother Mrs. Mehitabel Bramhall. Family NewsJoseph Bramhall to Bertha Bramhall1813-03-091
Scope and Contents
Brother Joseph Bramhall, Amenia, N.Y. to Dear Sister (Bertha Bramhall). Epidemic of sickness, family horseMoses Bramhall to Mehitabel Bramhall1814-02-251
Scope and Contents
Indenture between Moses Bramhall of Canaan, Columbia County, and Mehitabel Bramhall. Release of right of dowerRussell Cady to Elias W. Cady1817-03-10-1817-03-111
Scope and Contents
Russell Cady of Canaan to Respected Brother Elias W. Cady, Dryden. Selling of household goodsSilvina Bramhall to Mrs. Mehitabel Bramhall1818-12-271
Scope and Contents
Silvina Bramhall of Amenia, New York to Honored Mother Mrs. Mehitabel Bramhall. Family NewsA list of land owned by John C. Hogeboom1819-02-241Russell Cady to Elias W. Cady1819-10-211

Scope and Contents
John C. Hogeboom at Ghent to Elias W. Cady. Dryden PropertyRussell Cady to Elias W. Cady1820-05-251
Scope and Contents
Family NewsSilvina Bramhall to Mehitabel Bramhall1820-11-121
Scope and Contents
Silvina Bramhall of Dryden to Dear Mother Mehitabel Bramhall at Amenia. Family NewsMehitabel Bramhall to Silvina Bramhall1821-08-011
Scope and Contents
Mehitabel Bramhall to daughter Silvina Bramhall, Dryden. Family NewsRussell Cady to Elias W. Cady1821-08-121
Scope and Contents
Russell Cady at Canaan to brother Elias W. Cady, Dryden. Family illnessBetsey Delano to Mehitabel Bramhall1824-10-081
Scope and Contents
Betsey Delano to Mother Mehitabel Bramhall at New Canaan. Death of cousin EbenezerElias W. Cady to Aaron Konkle1834-02-051
Scope and Contents
Contract between Elias W. Cady, acting as agent for John C. Hogeboom, and Aaron Konkle concerning land in Elmira (then Tioga County: Chemung formed March 29, 1836. (Paid in full, June 1835)John C. Hogeboom to Elias W. Cady1834-05-131
Scope and Contents
Arrangement concerning sale of landRussell Cady to Elias W. Cady1835-01-081
Scope and Contents
Russell Cady to brother Elias W. Cady. Sheep farming techniquesJohn C. Hogeboom to Elias Cady1835-01-161
Scope and Contents
Business transactions, including purchase of sheepRussell Cady to Elias W. Cady1835-05-261
Scope and Contents
Russell Cady to brother Elias Cady. Market for wool, family newsJohn C. Hogeboom to Elias W. Cady1837-11-301
Scope and Contents
Sale of lot in town of Chemung, Tioga CountyElias to Russell1838-06-181
Scope and Contents
Elias at Dryden to brother Russell. Farming News (Unsigned or incomplete)John C. Hogeboom to Elias W. Cady1838-09-261
Scope and Contents
Sale of lot in Chemung, Tioga CountyColin Robinson to Elias Cady1843-06-011
Scope and Contents
Colin Robinson, Syracuse to Elias Cady. Shipment of sugar and molassesJohn Osburn to John Cady1849-10-251
Scope and Contents
Receipt for barley shipped by canal boat from John Cady for $205.81; signed John Osburn, New York CityLochlin to John Cady1849-10-251
Scope and Contents
Statement of barley bought by Mr. Lochlin from John Cady. New York CityRobert Cahoon1849-10-261
Scope and Contents
Receipt for barley delivered to brewery - Robert CahoonM. Goodrich to E.W. Cady1850-01-121
Scope and Contents
M. Goodrich, Dryden (Member of Congress, 1871-73) to Hon. E.W. Cady (NYS Assemblyman from Tompkins County, Dist. 2, 1850+) concerning New criminal and civil code - problems involved in reforming legal systemGarry Chambers to E.W. Cady1850-02-011
Scope and Contents
Garry Chambers of McLean, New York to Hon. E.W. Cady at Albany. Petition to repeal new school lawC.D. Hart to E.W. Cady1850-02-111
Scope and Contents
C.D. Hart of McLean (probably Charles; see Landmarks b. 1801 - d. 1880) to E.W. Cady. Opposition to school law, in favor of agriculture schoolE.P. Healy to E.W. Cady1850-02-281
Scope and Contents
E.P. Healy, Dryden to E.W. Cady. Opinion of county on school law, and agricultural college temperance as political question, scarlet fever epidemicH.W. Sage to E.W. Cady1850-03-091
Scope and Contents
H.W. Sage, Ithaca to Hon. E.W. Cady. Proposed Canal Bill (Sodus Canal) (see Landmarks, I, 37-38)J.B. Williams to E.W. Cady1850-03-161
Scope and Contents
J.B. Williams, Ithaca to Hon. E.W. Cady, Albany. Sodus Canal BillJohn Cady to E.W. Cady(?)1850-03-171
Scope and Contents
John Cady to (probably) E.W. Cady - envelope p.m. 3/20/50 to EWC, Albany. Home News an politicsHenry Brewer to E.W. Cady (?)1850-08-311
Scope and Contents
Henry Brewer of Enfield to (probably) E.W. Cady. Purchase of wheat drillE. Cornell to E.W. Cady18501
Scope and Contents
E. Cornell (Pres. of N.Y. & Erie Telegraph Co.), New York to Hon. E.W. Cady. Proposed law about liability of telegraph clerksH. Root to E.W. Cady1851-05-101
Scope and Contents
H. Root of Chatham to brother E. (E.W. Cady). BusinessJames Walker to Elias W. Cady1852-08-161
Scope and Contents
James Walker, Peace Dale, Rhode Island to Elias Cady. Purchase of wool.Jedediah Barber to Elias W. Cady1853-01-181
Scope and Contents
Jedidiah Barber of Homer, New York to Elias W. Cady. Business - transporting lumber by teamHorace Pomeroy to Elias Cady1853-03-171
Scope and Contents
Horace Pomeroy, Troy, Bradford County, Pa. to Elias Cady. Purchase of a mowing machine - is mowing machine practical?Horace Pomeroy to Elias Cady1853-06-081
Scope and Contents
Horace Pomeroy to Elias Cady. Purchase of mowing machine - Cady has agency for Ketchem's (Kitchen's) mower.J.R. Speed to E.W. Cady1853-09-281
Scope and Contents
J.R. Speed of Caroline to Hon. E.W. Cady. County Agricultural Fair - wants Cady to exhibit his "fine cattle and merino sheep."E.C. Bramhall to Elias W. Cady1854-04-071
Scope and Contents
E.C. Bramhall to Uncle Elias W. Cady. Obtaining a deed.John McGraw to E.W. Cady1856-08-271
Scope and Contents
John McGraw, New York City to E.W. Cady. Barley market prospectsE. Cornell to E.W. Cady1857-01-231
Scope and Contents
E. Cornell, N.Y.S. Assembly, Albany to Hon. E.W. Cady. Proposed increased tax on telegraph company.E. Cornell to E.W. Cady1857-03-091
Scope and Contents
E. Cornell, Ithaca to Hon. E.W. Cady. Proposing amendment to April 15, 1854 law to allow formation of a corporation to improve the breeding of short horn cattle.E.W. Cady to Oliver1857-03-121
Scope and Contents
E.W. Cady at Albany to Son Oliver. He has just talked with Dr. Elisha Harris of the Staten Island Marine Hospital who thinks EWC will help him keep his place; EWC thinks he can help the Cadys to find help, presumably from among immigrants ("…...a Dutch Girl would be what we should want....") KJ 6/24/1969Sheep Sold18611
Scope and Contents
Fragment - List of sheep sold.John Cady to E.W. Cadydate unknown1
Scope and Contents
John Cady to Father E.W. Cady. The Farm.Series II. Undated MaterialList of property in Broome, Cayuga, Seneca, Steuben, Tioga Countiesdate unknown1Publius Lentulus to Senate of Romedate unknown1
Scope and Contents
Copy of a description of the Person Jesus Christ - as it was found in an ancient manuscript sent by Publius Lentulus, President of Judea to the Senate of Rome; excerpt from 125th Psalm; sermon on Faith.Poem "The Exile"date unknown1Names on outside flapdate unknown1
Scope and Contents
Edmund Bramhall, Sally Hulbert, Clarisa Hulbert, Hariet HulbertPoem - an elegy on death of Bramhalldate unknown1Sermon - "Is the Holy Ghost God?"date unknown1Sermon - "Can the doctrine of election be proved from Scripture?"date unknown1Description of Giovanni de Medicidate unknown1 ======= Link to a partial finding aid.
